frameworks.name: delayed divisive normalization
frameworks.one_line: DN model augmented with category-dependent input scaling to predict adaptation.

frameworks.name: short-term neural adaptation
frameworks.one_line: Adaptation signatures characterized across the human visual hierarchy.

frameworks.name: category selectivity
frameworks.one_line: Preferred vs non-preferred category response differences in higher areas.

core_question: How do short-term neural adaptation patterns and mechanisms differ across the human visual hierarchy?
key_assumptions: Adaptation can be indexed via responses to varying stimulus duration and repetition interval
Divisive normalization with category-scaled input captures cross-area adaptation
iEEG time-varying signals reflect local cortical adaptation dynamics
key_findings: Ventral- and lateral-occipitotemporal cortex show slower, more prolonged adaptation than V1-V3
Recovery from repetition adaptation is slower in higher visual areas
For category-selective sites, recovery is slower for preferred than non-preferred stimuli
Category-scaled DN model accurately predicts responses across image categories
interpretations: Hierarchical adaptation differences arise from slower normalization dynamics in higher areas
Category selectivity modulates input strength entering normalization, shaping recovery
